reviews.review_text: Labeled as an introductory class though most assignments require you to have previous coding experience. She has a hard time teaching; she is full of knowledge but fails to express it to her students effectively. Her lectures are hard to follow and she tends to make several last minute changes to her assignments and their due dates.

Professor Najjar is pretty nice/calm overall, but it is hard to keep up during lectures as she stumbles over her words a lot, lots of pauses and stuttering. It is pretty frustrating trying to understand and follow along. She also makes lots of mistakes, typos and things. TAs also expect you to know the material when you need their help.
Barely made it out with a B. lucky to not have an F. massive difficulty jump from test 1 to 2. Her teaching is like a middle schoolers who can't teach.

Dr. Najjar makes computer science very accessible for beginners who want to learn. 60% of the grade comes from three exams and a final, the grading structure has enough flexibility that one bad test won't ruin your grade. There's a big jump in difficulty after test 2, so stay engaged. Attend labs, put in some effort, and an A is very achievable.

Take this class if you can self teach the concepts to yourself and rely only on the textbook. You go into the labs and the TA's expects you to know what you read on the textbook. If you are not good in coding  I do not recommend Prof. Najjar. It is not really an introductory course with this professor. No opportunities of extra credit or retakes.

Avoid if possible. Practice quizzes don't align with actual exam material, making prep difficult. Feedback is extremely slow; grades went unposted for two months, leaving me unsure of my standing between an A or a C. It was a discouraging experience because the instructor provided very little helpful guidance throughout the term.

Dr. Wilson is the greatest professor I have ever taken at UNC Charlotte. She always does their best to support students wherever they can. Super often she (or her TAs) lets students turn assignments in late if they need to, and she's always super understanding. Labs are kinda unorganized but super helpful if you pay attention.

This class was not good. I couldn't find any time to focus on my other courses and most of the learning was conceptual, not practical. She mostly just shows you some slides, has you do a quiz, then sends you out to do code and projects without truly understanding it. The Course Canvas is also unorganized. I will say the TAs were good.
she great-kisses

Terrible. Time wasted in class forcing participation, no real study material for tests, no learning material(she posts two videos at the start of the week made by another professor) never reviews this material in class. Tests contain content never seen or discussed in class. TA's making a fraction of her salary are more beneficial to my learning.

For the majority of the class, you will be presenting and watching others present slides in groups made based on articles you can just find online. There is also a term project with confusing requirements, told to choose something you are passionate about and would interest him. You can do all of this without taking the class and spending tuition.

Professor has a habit of walking up to students and bothering them, oftentimes when they're trying to work. This semester he is also incredibly late on our final grades. The due date for grades was supposed to be May 12 and we still have not gotten the grades for our final projects. This class is quite easy, but this professor will annoy you.

Pretty good professor. He is an older guy, so his style can be a bit out of touch but if you really pay attention it shouldn't be too hard. Kind of bad at explaining any problems you have though, but will also push you to try and achieve more than you thought, and pushes you to go further outside the class. Very nice and understanding.

For backend software engineering, you will definitely learn a lot. Her lecture videos are posted on canvas and are very easy to follow. The assignments are just challenging enough to build your proficiency with Node.js and Layered Architecture, without being overwhelming. She also clearly cares about her students and is very nice to talk to.
Professor Cao does not play.
Video lectures are long and excessive with very little teachings. The final project criteria was excessive and unrealistic regarding how deliverables should be contributed to.
Professor is pretty bad at her video lectures. Don't take her if you already have no interest in the topic.

I signed up for this course over the summer, even though she wasn't the instructor for my section. The canvas videos were all made by her. This course was laid out as an end to end tutorial which is bad in my opinion, there is no critical thinking in this course. Probably the easiest A ive ever had. That being said, she does explain things well.

Class is incredibly disorganized, and accent is difficult to understand. Lectures are all over the place and she never finishes on time. Assignments are kinda random and vary a lot in time/difficulty. 1 midterm that was only worth 10% of the grade. She grades attendance but it's not worth a ton.

Shes not a bad professor but her class needs more clarity, it is often disorganized. She has so many little things to cover everyday but never actually finish in class. She will randomly make assignments optional. The course content is good and useful, though the delivery isnt very good. The assignments also very alot in terms of difficulty.

A lot of assignments that can get annoying. That being said the absolute best prof for this class. The beginning is hard but later on it gets a lot easier. Class is 5x harder with any other prof. She re-teaches the basics of DSA so don't feel like you will get lost in advanced stuff (basically dsa 1.5 not really 2.0)

This course focuses on Databases, primarily using SQL. While there were several HWs due each week, the instructions and grading were very straight forward. Lectures did not typically last long, and often times ended with her helping with HW assignments. The workload in the class was more than many of my others, but the difficulty was low.

She is hands down the worst professor here at UNC Charlotte. Do not take this class if you actually want to learn something. This class has felt like a scam since day 1. Her accent is also extremely thick and if you are unfamiliar with it you likely will have a hard time understanding her. Poor wording on most of if not all tests/quizzes.

PTA's grade harsh, they are not great at feedback, and don't answer questions in any helpful way. Grading is very slow, so for the amount of homework, you're often repeating mistakes because you haven't gotten a grade for similar assignments back. If I took this in person with a different professor, I don't think it would be this bad.
Great professor. He is very aware of current trends and practical skills in software engineering. Classwork was not overly difficult but was conducive to learning.

the professor is really nice and the class so far has been easier than i thought. i was worried because he had such a low rating on here, but i think they changed the curriculum because the course hasn't been super difficult at all or work heavy, and i'm still learning a lot. overall would absolutely recommend this professor!

Bunch of extra credit. he is a chill prof. TAs do the grading and half the labs are already done for you. This guy on zoom is so free. The concepts are light and the prof dosen't care for most things which is lit haha. Attendence is taken like once a month and is a quiz where you put the word he said out loud. Freest section you can take for 3155

Took this class online during the summer. Had zero interaction with the professor, the TAs do all the grading. The course however is basically all slideshows with a brief introduction to software engineering topics. Several quizzes and an assignment due every week. The group project was sort of disorganized, easy A regardless.

My grandmother mother has been really sick. Dr. Sahoo has been really caring, helpful and supportive. I have 4 more classes this semester. No other professors has been such supportive. She is a great human being, listens to your problems and find out a way to help you succeed. She has deep command over the material. Great class

Very caring and understanding professor. I am having some personal issues, she has been consistently supportive. Lecture are brief and amazing. She mentions interview questions in classroom discussions which is really helpful. Take her if you can

I just had couple of classes with her, what an absolute pleasure to learn from her! In the first class of learning, I was not able to figure out few things, she sat with me, explaining me each steps patiently. I have never had such experience in CLT. Next class, again I observed her going over the steps with some folks diligently. Rare to find!

Honestly one of the better professors I've had. Explains concepts clearly and actually cares if students understand, not just memorize. Lectures are structured, organized, and make hard topics feel manageable.

Workload is fair . you have to put in effort, but it's never busywork. Exams are reasonable if you keep up with class and practice.

Very approachable and supportive. Will help if you ask questions and genuinely wants students to succeed. Also flexible and understanding when life happens (rare but appreciated).

If you show up for code review pay attention, and do the work, you'll be fine. Would recommend 100%.

1213 is a HUGE class, so you will only interact with Professor Whaley if you put in the effort to do so. Caring professor, confusing class structure. I had to guesstimate my grade the entire semester using RapidTables. Easy exams but you have to put in the work to self-learn.

Mr. Whaley mostly leads the online lectures once a week and leaves the labs to the TAs, so there isn't as much interaction with him. Although you don't interact with him as much, his online lectures are very entertaining, and he's very lenient with homework. The assignments themselves are not too hard, especially with previous knowledge of Java

Good professor and made it relatively easy to do well in the class. He provides extra credit opportunities and options to retake exams. I didn't do well on all of the exams, but found my grade to still stand at an A. Make sure to do the textbook assignments, show up to labs/lectures + TA review sessions, and do the module quizzes as MCQ prep!

Professor offers little real help and his online explanations feel random and unorganized. If you rely on your instructor to actually teach, don't take this class. The TAs are the only real support. The course is very confusing at the start, even experienced students struggle. I do not recommend. Save yourself the stress and choose wisely.

He is willing to help students if that is necessary like if you have technical issues but he's a professor you hardly see but he is there and he also cares about making sure you can do well in the class.

Absolutely appalling that this professor is still allowed to "teach", if you can even call it that. Zero lectures, Zero reading, just stolen content from youtube. Vague, confusing, and boring assignments that we were forced to use AI on because he taught us the programming language that the assignments were supposed to be done in.

Each project has an outdated and poor outline of every step that has to be taken to complete it. Prerequisites don't cover what has to be known and he doesn't go over it. He is also very strict on attendance even though he never teaches. He would VERY reluctantly open a poll everywhere for another 15 seconds if you miss the narrow window it's open.

To say he teaches is an overstatement that would make even Peter Molyneux blush. This professor does not teach you how to do anything, he instead opts to just give you 8 pages of instructions and let you figure it out. He responds to genuine issues with sarcasm, and he does not even seem like he wants to be there. Pass.

I have taken a few courses with Dr. Mejias and he consistently sets clear expectations with regards to due dates, what will be on tests, and where students may get stuck on assignments and how to overcome those issues.

He has a good grasp of the material. And also provides test reviews prior to exams.

This class was a joke. We were told first day that we "weren't going to be doing any coding" in a software development class... He then assigned us 5 projects, the last one consisting of 4 parts, and that was basically the whole class. But he would give us answers to literally everything except the final (you can use AI). Easiest A I've ever had.

I took this class in the Fall and the professor is great. His accent is good, and he understands the subject. He strictly adheres to this book - "Neal Davis - AWS Practice Questions" (kindle version). for all questions. He really wants his students to get the AWS certification (CLF-02 exam). I would recommend taking him for THIS course atleast.

Great at lecturing, but the exams are full of “gotcha” questions (mean: 61, median: 64). In the last few weeks he ran out of material, so class turned into watching student presentations at 1.5x speed. We still didn't get through everyone, and it cost us our entire final review session. It's obvious his heart is in research rather than teaching.

Requires HTB access code ($300) for class and won't budge. HTB is managed by TAs who consistently let you down. Independent HTB done prior as a student doesn't count towards this grade, he said the only reason was because of the deal he's got with HTB. Very little guidance offered, but the class content itself is very interesting.

Professor relies heavily on an outdated Google Doc syllabus that is very hard to navigate. Lectures consist of 2 hrs of boring content and sometimes explaining assignments. Textbook is a $300 HTB subscription that is weighted more than other assignments. Exams are alright but not much content to study. Great guy and knowledgeable not good teacher.

I had a good experience with this professor.  The projects are not too difficult, and he will demo the solution or most of the solution in class before the project is due.  He is a little disorganized, but everything is present on the syllabus.  The midterm and final are really difficult and do not line up well with the quizzes and projets.

This demanding course required building about two mobile apps per week and watching lectures. It focused on Android and iOS, letting students choose their platform. Industry-focused assignments supported self-paced learning and helped me build a strong app portfolio that performed well in internship interviews.

This course was very demanding, requiring me to build about two mobile apps per week and spend time watching the instructor's YouTube lectures. The workload was intense but rewarding. I learned a lot and have done well in internship interviews by showcasing a strong portfolio of apps built in this class.

The class was on mobile development with a focus on Java and XML languages. The workload is extensive with up to 2 assignments per week, however they are not difficult. The lectures are all online, while class time is spent working on homework assignments. The tests and assignments are all similar to his lecture videos.

Professor Shehab is an outstanding educator who integrates his software development expertise into the classroom. I appreciated his YouTube videos and the active learning methods he employed during lessons. Be prepared to put in the effort and master coding skills.

Professor is an exceptional instructor with a hands-on teaching approach and excellent instructional videos that make complex concepts easy to grasp. His focus on real-world applications has been invaluable in my day-to-day job as a software engineer. The projects I completed in his class helped me build a portfolio that led to my summer internship
There are surprise attendance checks and pop quizzes. Besides that, he is a good instructor, kind of funny, and is very open to helping however he can. Also a semi-heavy accent.

The professor is kind, but the class organization was all over the place. Easily the most disorganized course I've ever taken. No one I know who has taken 2181 has had a good experience. If you have to take this course for your major, good luck.
Heavy accent but good professor. He cares and will help

Flipped-style classroom, with slideshows gone over again in class. He is very open to re-explaining topics and truly wants his students to succeed. This class is not light in work, but there are plenty of lecture videos, coding videos, samples, and slides you can refer to. Great teacher!

The tests are difficult since they're all hand-written, and I often cut it pretty close to the timer even coming in with a lot of experience and studying beforehand. You definitely need to know your stuff, but Dr. Yan gives you plenty of resources and opportunities to learn it and his lectures are great. Definitely recommend.

I really enjoyed this class and thought Professor Shin was an excellent teacher. Nothing about the class was particularly difficult, however there are a lot of assignments to keep up with every week. Seems like he cares about his students and really enjoys teaching cs.

Easy class with almost no work. All your work is done in assigned groups so if you get a group you don't like, it might be tougher. The professor is very laid back but he expects you to try. He genuinely wants you to succeed but expects you to put in a little work.

This professor was very engaging and wanted us to succeed since public speaking is an important skill to master. As for the work, it's not difficult since the majority of it is group work. You are graded on very few things, so make sure to submit the best version you have, since, in my case, a bad grade will bring your grade down.

Most of the work in the class is done in predetermined groups so if you get stuck with people who don't show up or care to participate you are screwed. Showing up on time and participating is the majority of the grade. If you get a bad group it will drag your grade down as he does group grades not individual.

The professor was very hands-off, didn't really teach anything, you just do discussions/papers/power points, which are based on a vague prompt you have to research on your own. It was a gen-ed, so I guess that's a good thing. Very easy class if you don't mind writing a lot.

His class is an interesting one . Be ready since attendance is mandatory 
The class is competitive and requires interaction with classmates since there's a project given to groups the beginning of the class so does what you do the whole semester.
It's a great class .

Overall really bad. around 75 percent of us failed a test bc she didn't prepare us for it said their were different questions on it then what their actually were,and didn't give us a practice test until two days before I know 75 percent of us failed bc she gives us a class grade breakdown She also wouldn't curve the grade DO NOT TAKE her class

This is not an easy elective. You have to learn a lot of vocabulary and there are too many readings on ZyBooks that you must memorize for in-person quizzes. The teacher is alright, but it's just too much homework. Cheat sheets could make up for all the memorization though.

she was a good professor and gave great extra credit at the end of the semester. she has a few problems with her speech but it didn't matter too much. i think if i studied more i could've gotten a higher grade but i still ended with an A and didn't really put a lot of effort in.

She is flexible on attendance and grades but there are a lot of presentations and Labs she stutters a bit at times. As long as you complete the labs, discussion post, and final project on time this class will be fairly easy to understand The midterm and final were a bit long but she does give an option for a cheat sheet DONT skip a lab assignment

flexible. lots of presentations. lowkey had an annoying voice. the midterm and final were easy ! the project was fairly easy. just lots of really tedious labs and lectures weren't helpful. gave extensions if needed

online class but lots of group works and group projects. in-class and prep work is worth most of grade. assigns the same amount of readings and prep for grads and undergrads, so if your undergrad, it feels like this class is much more of my week then my other normal undergrad classes. Also only offered at night.

Physical Computing is probably my favorite course so far and Dr. Wilson is def 1 of my favorite profs! Take his class if you want to make neat stuff! There's a decent amount of work and it's hard to catch up if you fall behind, but Dr. Wilson is very helpful with questions if you struggle with a concept. Idk why the bad reviews, he's a great prof!

The professor was pleasant, but this class was effectively teaching you to use the worst possible tool for a given task. We would spend hours building low-fidelity, inefficient artifacts that would probably get you fired from a workplace if you got caught building on the clock. You complete dozens of assignments to produce unimpressive products.

Only thing I like about this class: During group assignments, you're only responsible for your portion. This week I have a project, quiz prep work 3x, and in-class work. It's the same thing EVERY week, besides the project and prep work is usually 2-1x. He hides your overall grade. Acting like I don't have 4 other classes. Very time-consuming :/

I absolutely loved this course and, though there is a lot of group projects and work, I felt like I learned a lot from it all that I'm still using today. His class is also very well-organized and was just a lot of fun. I would absolutely take a class with him again!

I am in my third year at this university and this has been the worst class I have ever taken. Get ready to teach yourself because the professor and the TAs will not reply to emails. I sent an email regarding a command that did not work and the professor simply said "the command did not work". The TAs also took days to respond.

Grading felt unclear and inconsistent. TAs handled most grading, and regrade requests went back to the same grader, so there was no fresh review. I would have preferred more professor involvement and clearer feedback. Students who value transparency just stay away.

Great professor who makes the class fun and interesting. It can get overwhelming near the end with an essay, test and final game project, but staying on top of the work keeps it manageable. Highly recommended for future game devs. He gives a lot of extra credit. The tests are the hardest part but with the test reviews the TAs run, it makes it easy.

Bahamon is a great lecturer, the tests were very hard though and he didn't really give any specific study guide, they had a lot of vocabulary that you just need to study for straight up. He's very understanding though, if you talk to him about something, he'll be up front about it.
Decent professor who cares about his students. That being said, the tests are hard and he puts a lot of weight on them.

I loved having Dr Ahmed for Artificial Intelligence (6150) and for Data Structures (6114). If you are trying to float through the class do not take him. He is very passionate about helping his students learn and is very purposeful with the way he teaches and the way he gives activities. Very hands on course work and many opportunities to succeed!

you will get sure A+ if you have access to his old questions. And his TAs are always indians, I saw they help the students who are indian in their own language during exams and i did not like that. anyways the grading was easy if you did everything right in assignments and projects.

ITCS 6114 is a data structures and algorithms based class. While no coding is conducted in the class, well over 30 different algorithms are discussed in great detail. The workload in the class is not heavy, There were not many HW assignments, test, or projects. However grading was very strict, and failing 1 assignment or test could lead to failing

Him being part of the class is a waste of time. The lectures is just reading off of slides verbatim. Class project is just busy work and strung together so badly that you are left confused as to what you have to do. Really needs to update his material it is severely outdated. Probably just another research professor who does not care. 100% avoid.

Does not teach well if at all. Just reads the slides. A good idea of how he lectures:
This is our problem, now this is how to solve it, *clicks slide* there thats how we did it.
Does not explain anything well and I'm not even sure he is doing some things right. I saw his same problem on youtube and they both had different answers. Stay away.

I appreciated his relaxed yet effective style. Instead of traditional lectures, he guided us through a hands‑on project, gave clear, constructive feedback through weekly meetings, and fostered a supportive atmosphere. Expectations were fair, deadlines reasonable, and grading transparent. Highly recommend!

Dobbs gives great feedback and is readily available, while being very hands-off. His
industry experience, adaptability, and openness to all feedback help students feel engaged. 

While the structure and grading can be slightly confusing, he listens and improves quickly, making the learning experience pleasant. Effort and asking questions= easy A/B!
The professor gives too much work and often time does not understand what he is teaching himself. Going to ask for help to him is like talking to a stone, nothing useful at all.
Prof. Dobbs was really helpful whenever we as a group or I individually had any questions

Professor Dodds emphasizes practical understanding over rote memorization and encourages consistent attendance, participation, and submission of work with strong grades. In ITSC 3155, he designs the class and content to closely mirror real-world computing environments.
Homework assignments are long, tedious, and don't really teach you anything.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!! 

If you can wait another semester to take it or you have a second option take that and don't look back. 

His homework is extremely complicated (even his TAs get confused), he doesn't go over any of the coding in class - just rambles about unrelated stuff

This is easily the worst professor/class I have ever had at UNCC

Nice man. Terrible class. This was by far one of the worst and hardest classes I have ever taken. The material in this class is so unnecessary and so confusing. Instructions are unclear and confusing. This class is basically in a foreign language.

Professor Wartell is an awful professor, he left the class a halfway through the semester and had another professor teach his class. He had no TA and had trouble trying to grade our first assignments. We never received any of our grades for the assignments until we had to bombard the TA of the other professor for grades. This class is unnecessary

There are 2 entries referring to the same guy on here. Hands down, best prof/mentor I've had. Gives great lectures and relevant advice to CS students pursuing dev careers. Yes, he goes fast but he records everything so if you miss something just watch the video (duh)? Absolutely take any course he teaches if you actually want a future in tech.

He teaches at a very fast pace, which makes it impossible to follow along during in-class demos.  The code that he teaches is not in the lecture slides and there is no textbook.  If you did not write down the code in class, you must either page through the entire recorded lecture video, or look it up online.  There is a huge push to teach yourself.

Krevart is okay but he lacks compassion, badly. Every week, he teaches you something new and expects you to have complete mastery over it. So either you do an assignment "right" or a HUGE chunk of your grade disappears, courtesy of his TAs who are very eager to take points off for minor mistakes. If you learn by trial and error, then avoid him.
Great professor, teaches well and actually gives real life examples. Can tell he cares about students and wants to set them up for success.

Easily the best professor in the CCI department at UNCC. It's not even a competition. The final project for this class forces you to make a project using the same skills and tools used by full-stack devs in the industry. It could also help boost your resume. The TAs are also incredibly skilled and knowledgeable about the class.

I took ITSC 2600 (Computing Professionals) as a transfer class. Professor Slane is very kind and understanding. There's one weekly reflection and occasional assignments. Guest speakers visit weekly and share useful resources and advice for student success. She also responds to emails quickly. Easy class with a good teacher who cares about students.

Ms. Slane's class is a great class to come in for 50 minutes to relax and listen to speakers from the multiple departments here on campus and from companies. Yes, sometimes the lectures can be boring, but for only 50 minutes they're not too bad. The work itself is easy, but make sure to do the assignments, so that you're not stuck with a B like me.

Lectures were basically ppl coming from different departments and talking. Attendance was taken thru pollev. Homework was light work, occasional discussion post or reflection. Had a final group project that we had plenty of time to work on and was fairly easy to do. Professor was nice and seemed to care about her students and their success.
She is the nicest teacher ever, and her class is very easy. Class can be a little boring, but only 50 mins.

ITSC 1600 is an easy course. The class mainly consists of different presenters from student organizations, companies, campus resources, etc. There are some interesting/useful lectures, but a majority of them are quite boring. The assignments are not based on any of the lectures but are relatively simple. Attendance was 10%, and there was no final.

By far the easiest class I have taken at UNC Charlotte. It was very self-directed and self-taught but the content wasn't challenging. Because everything was online (including test + final) and attendance was not taken you could easily pass this class with an A without ever showing up.

Professor Jugan is easy-going, straightforward, and informative. Attendance is optional and he even takes the courtesy of recording the lectures. This course has few assignments that aren't difficult at all, but it still feels like I learned a lot. A big plus of professor Jugan is that he's easy to reach outside of class!

I took this class on Zoom, attendance is optional and lectures are recorded. This is a project based course, you get to decide what you make. Since you decide, he doesn't give much guidance but is available for questions during class or office hours. There is very minimal work with this class, it's an easy technical elective.

Professor Jugan is a really good professor. He helps you understand the concept of unity and how to code a game from scratch. He is a leineit grader and he is very helpful. We had 4 homework assignments that stacked each time and we had the final game to present as our final.

He's a good choice for intro to AI. Super knowledgeable in the games industry and talks a lot about his experience. The study guides he provides are extremely helpful for the midterm and the final. There are 3 projects in the course. Attend class or watch the class recordings to get good insight on how to do them.

Don't recommend at all, especially online. Lectures are near incomprehensible and very difficult to follow, and usually never line up with the projects you are working on. You are expected to be good at C# and Unity, or have AI do all the coding. Midterm was set up to be near impossible. Not nearly enough time to complete any of it.

No clear instructions, difficulty in communication, terrible grading, no teaching, 0 understanding. 
You won't learn anything, arbitrary grading, no clear rubric followed while grading assignments. You will waste your time, better take difficult class than wasting your time on this. If the university is listening, please get rid of such classes.
An easy A at the cost of your sanity

I've taken Dr. Benedict for data mining and data visualizations, and she was my advising professor for my senior capstone project. Never in my life have I had a professor more relatable and understanding. Her classes have motivated me to change my career path towards data analytics, and as a student I could not recommend her enough.

She is very relaxed, no attendance. Her class felt like a youtube tutorial, there is very little content and everything can be easily found online for free. She speaks for about 15 minutes over some AI generated slides or the TAs will have a code demo, and the rest of the class you do whatever. This class was a waste of time and money.

She's young and fresh out of grad school, which means that she's really chill and understanding. It seems like she cares, but she doesn't actually have much control over the structure of the course. Typically generates assignments with AI. It seems like she's typically busy and doesn't devote much to the class. New to teaching and too slow.
Great for 2214, would recommend if you want a relaxed professor overall, go with graduate students over professors.

I've taken data structures under two other professors and she is by far the best.  If you get the chance this is the professor you want to teach you data structures if you truly want to understand what you're learning!

The Prof. was absent the entire semester. I feel like the TAs ran the class most of the time. The material was not too difficult to learn. If you don't have a good team, you will have to work extra hard to get an A. The class is writing intensive. You don't do front-end design in this class but you learn how/why you should design in certain ways.

Dr. Ras is very knowledgeable and very kind to students. Unfortunately he is just not a good lecturer. Students used to pack the room when his TA showed up for exam reviews. He is accessible during office hours and will give you a chance to make up for mistakes if you participate regularly. Only 2-3 exams will make up your entire grade.

I took 3 classes with him and his teaching is amazing. He's really good at explaining complex topics in a simple way with real-world examples. His classes and even recorded lectures are engaging. He encourages participation, invites industry speakers, and is very kind and supportive during class and exams. He is my favorite professor in uncc.

I took 3 classes with him and his teaching is amazing. He's really good at explaining complex topics in a simple way with real-world examples. His classes and even recorded lectures are engaging. He encourages participation, invites industry speakers, and is very kind and supportive during class and exams. He is my favorite professor in uncc.

The professor receives more criticism than deserved. He is very knowledgeable about the material and has a great sense of humor that makes lectures engaging. He encourages students to ask questions and is happy to clarify concepts. The class is enjoyable and rewarding if you stay focused and put in the effort.

Overall class is on visual optics using AI. Homework and projects are all coded in Python. He gives plenty of extra credit, and chances to make up grades. Bonus points were on almost all quizes, and the ability to re-do assignments were always available. No final, but a final group project is assigned
Just avoid the class. Dr Das is knowledgeable but has weird sense of humor and god complex attitude.

A profoundly confusing structure that meets homework wholly unrelated to what's taught in class. If you have taken Intro to ML get ready to relearn it for half the course instead of what you signed up for. The class has no actual rigor and can easily be completed, but don't expect to actually get the intuition behind the topic from the classroom.

Amazing professor that really enjoys teaching students. He really cares about students understanding what he is teaching and makes complex topics really easy to understand. She will never respond to her emails, doesn't care about her students and you will not learn anything from her class. NEVER TAKE ANY OF HER CLASSES

oh yes Srinivas akella is the best!!!!!!

Professor Vainberg's Calc 3 class can be challenging, but attending lectures and using resources like Unstuckstudy can help. Stay on top of the WebWork assignments and you'll do well. Overall, a decent class if you put in the effort.

This class was harder than it needed to be. He relies to much on textbook reading to be lecturing with no planning. Consistently got problems wrong. I made a RMP account just to give this guy a good rating. He is extremely caring and understanding. He is a quiet speaker and has an accent. It's kind of difficult to read his handwriting so you're gonna have to teach yourself. He gives 3 tests. He's very nice but impossible to reach outside of class and doesn't answer emails.

I went to every class, took notes, did the homework, and every test is worded differently than what he gives you to practice. He then wonders why the average score is a 65. Also, I know he tries his best, but you CANNOT UNDERSTAND WHAT HE IS SAYING

Can barely understand anything, tests aren't reflective of what's in class OR on the practice exam. He words it all differently, then wonders why the average test score is a 65. Don't take this class if you want to pass Stat.
Very difficult lectures and beware of the test also very hard.

I saw the 2.5 and expected him to be a super hard professor however he is actually amazing. dont take this guy bro. i feel like ive been waterboarded when i walk out of his class. i cnat understand the terrible accent, and bro cant answer a question to save his life. there is no avail in bro, only pain

It was a great math class. Professor Reed cares a lot about her students and everyones success. I had a lot of fun in her class

Reed is a good professor but her class is unorganized. She gave us a course calendar at the beginning of the year which I just follow my test dates (the only thing she sticks too). Great teacher, all lectures were very well organized and easy to follow. Tests were slightly difficult.

At first I thought she was a bit scatter-brained but as the semester when on, I understood why she teaches the way she does. VERY BIG ON GROUP WORK!! Each month you will have an exam on the unit and you will do well if you take notes and study before each test, even if you're not the best at math.

Such a fun and easy math class. If you like realistic math that isn't super heavy on formulas and complex equations, this class is for you. Professor Birdsong is super sweet and there is lots of extra credit opportunities.

Pretty good professor, never really had problems with her, which says a lot considering how rude a lot of UNCC professors are. We had an easy course so I rarely had to ask for help. She provides plenty of resources and teaches the class pretty well

Honestly, he's not a bad professor. The only problem for real is his accent. It can be hard to understand, but he uses a doc cam for his note taking so I just copied everything he wrote down and that got me through the class. The exam was hard though.

His accent is hard to understand at first, but gets easier through the semester. All of his quizzes are online and the textbook is optional but recommended to be used like homework. He has 3 tests and a final that make up most of your grade.

Amazing Professor! Don't believe what anyone else writes (they didn't try), if you take the time to practice his practice exams you will always get an A. Practice exams are word for word what will be on the test. Don't be shy to talk to him! He cares about all the students and amazing to talk to!

I really enjoyed this class and he is an amazing teacher who sets you up for success. Only downside is the attendance policy, you can only have 4 absences. Other than that, if you study and reach out the the TA or professor then you can make it through with a pretty good grade.

Lots of Hawkes homework, but they're easy 100%'s. It's also mandatory in the curriculum for the university so that won't change depending on professors. She's very clear and personable. This professor gives great lectures, attendance almost every class, great professor that actually teaches calculus, ive never taken any kind of calc and she does a great job explaining everything.
Caylah Crosby is one of the best professor's I have ever had in years of schooling. I'm not good at mathematics but she is very engaging, helpful and kind.

I really enjoyed taking Calculus with Professor Crosby. She is very supportive and takes her time to ensure students truly understand the material. Her teaching style is clear, patient, and effective. Take her if you can !

Professor Crosby is the best. She treats every problem like you've never seen it before and it helps you learn the concepts so much better. There is a lot of homework, but the tests are pretty easy. Terrible lectures, weird grading, and a heavy accent with terrible take-home notes. Would not recommend to take unless you can learn the pretty difficult material from just the textbook

Difficult to understand in class. I've had very good teachers with thick accents but Sonin specifically mumbles and is hard to hear. Most of your grade is rare homework problems which you can only solve by looking up how to do them.
Was very hard to follow during lectures as his notes were structured differently and was very confusing. You are better off teaching yourself the textbook as to not get confused by his teaching.

Difficult to understand. His accent and handwriting are too difficult to follow. His lectures are printed out handwritten notes. I had to drop this class because he was too difficult to follow. She's a nice lady and can be helpful at times but I wouldn't recommend her. She's hard to understand in class and is unable to answer a lot of questions. Dr. Jutmaan is a sweetheart. That being said, her lectures are ineffective and leave you with weekly pre class assignments/videos for learning core concepts. Dr. J always re-explained statistic concepts (with an understandable accent). Watch Canvas videos and do the Realizeit assignments while taking notes because those will really help you in the 5 exams (including the common final). She has a very heavy accent and speaks very quickly, but she will slow down if you ask her to and is very open to answering questions. This was more about learning Microsoft excel than it was doing math, which made it that much harder. Many quizzes and tests(4 and a final exam) which are a big portion of your grade.
She is not good at speaking English and she doesn't teach you the material you have to watch videos at home to learn the material
Lessons with clear, understandable, thoughtful notes. Makes the content seem easy, though it is still difficult. Little grades: 3 HW, 3 Test.
BEST TEACHER!!!

No one is as straightforward when it comes to explaining problems as Dr. Safronov. Differential Equations is not an inherently easy class but Dr. Safronov makes it feel simple. Cannot recommend him enough.

He explains the topics clearly and goes over the problems in detail. He usually goes over what problems are going to be on the exam the lecture before the exam which is helpful. Three HW assignments, three exams, and one final. Truly one of the best math professors I've taken!

This class was a waste of time; lectures were so easy to the point I didn't even pay attention. Literally the easiest A ever. His lectures were pointless. His lectures are long but oh well thats every class. This guy is awesome and hes the nicest professor I've ever had. You can tell he loves his job and actually cares about the students. Professor Wantland was one of the best math teachers I've ever had! Not the worst option in the department but not the best. His lectures are really the only bright spot from my experience. With how many tests/quizzes are given keeping a good grade is definitely tough, especially since none are dropped.

Very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very good professor

If you complete the homework and quizzes early, you will receive extra credit. Tests count as a large portion of your grade though you shouldn't fail them if you at least study.
Now I can't prove to you that God is real, but I can prove to you that Robert Bland is the greatest math professor of all time.
Amazing professor, I should have spent more time studying outside of class for the exams.

Professor Daher was overall a great teacher. He is willing to help with anything and shows you how to do it by hand and calculator. Your grade is made up of 4 tests, homework, and a final. Professor Daher was a great professor. He was very clear on every subject matter and used many example similar to his tests.
Good professor, just make sure you show up to class and pay attention and you will be fine!

The lectures are nothing like the homework for the most part, and you have to learn a lot by yourself. The only pro is that the test and quiz questions are structured exactly like the homework, so MEMORIZE how to do the problems. There is also no partial credit, which sucks.

Can be confusing but will make sure you understand if you ask questions, will also go out of his way do extra examples if asked.

Dr. Striplin says a lot of information in lectures that you are expected to remember, so I recommend always taking notes and focusing on aleks. Make sure to study multiple days for tests and take go to TA sessions, or you won't pass this class.

I have always been good at chemistry. I passed AP chem with flying colors and skipped gen chem 1 because of that, however with the way she lectured, I couldn't grasp a thing. I had better luck copying and interpreting notes from my peers. This class is tough and I didn't feel like she cared to help me grasp topics when I struggled. Not fun for me.

Chem 1252 is a challenging course, but Mrs. Michaels makes getting a C or higher achievable. As long as you watch the PCA videos and attend the lectures, you will pass the course. She is very understanding and provides a lot of materials to ensure you pass the course. Overall, if you have to take Chem 1252, this is the professor you want to have.

You basically have to teach yourself everything. Class time is only 50 minutes, which is way too short for the amount of information being taught. Her lectures mainly focus on what the concepts are rather than practicing the math problems that mainly show up on the tests. She really likes to throw curveballs on the tests!!!
Worst for Lab. Changes due dates with

Worst lab instructor. Never helps when I have a genuine problem, and always puts the blame on the students even when the TA or herself makes a mistake. Labs aren't graded until 2 weeks later, no makeup or extra credit. Never lets you go over the lab to understand what you missed, rushes material, and worst of all changes due dates without notice.

Being completely honest this class is a bit difficult but Dr. Horger makes the class a bit more tolerable as he does alot of examples in class. He is really nice and does take time to answer any questions that you may have. Also some advice is his exams and weekly quizzes are very similar to the problems in the textbook so he does rely on it alot.

Truly the glue holding UNCC's chem dept together. He's a great prof + definitely teaches w/ a lot of structure, which my ADHD brain needed to pass this class on the 3rd try. He definitely expects you to put in effort outside of class, but in return he is patient, charmingly dorky, & open to all questions. Do the reading & problems before class!

I ended up withdrawing from the class because I picked up an overnight job, but I truly wish I could have stayed. I asked a lot of questions- he never had any problem doing extra problems to explain, and he always broke it down in a digestible way that made it clear to me. He was incredibly patient and cared about student success. Gonna miss it

I had Dr. Horger for CHEM 2131 and I did not like him but in CHEM 2132 I felt more engaged and his lectures were much clearer and structured. He does not do exams, only a quiz every other week. Pay attention in class and do the homework assigned and you will do fine in his class. He is a harder professor however it prepares you for the ACS final.

This is simply a difficult class! He's a great professor but you have to put in the work, know your stuff, and you CANNOT fall behind. Read the textbook! He gives a ton of practice problems from there and shares previous quizzes (no keys). Your grade is 65% 8 quizzes (lowest gets dropped), 25% cumulative final & 10% homework/in class assignments.

PLS for the love of God, don't take him. If you think you will make it, he will prove you wrong. All the bad thing said on him is just the tip of the iceberg. Save yourself from a withdrawal of a failed grade for yourself and your sanity.

Dr Etzkorn's class is more difficult than other teacher's 2131 classes. If you care about chemistry and want to learn the content, take him. Lots of homework. If you show up to office hours he will try to help you. He offers a lot of extra credit. I spent about 20 hours / week studying and doing the homeworks. Do all the homework, it helps!

A lot of what is said about him on here is true. His class is very difficult and you are graded essentially on three tests and a final exam (all compose about 75% of your final grade.) With that, you only need a 60% to make a C and, for the students who didn't withdraw, he gave a very generous 100 points of extra credit assignments.

Unfortunately this professor expects his students to dedicate a ridiculous amount of time to his class every single week, please take this into consideration for your schedule. I dropped the class because of all the homework assigned and his expectation of students to fully understand the material before lecture (then why have class?).

Dr. Etzkorn is the only professor I ever warn people about. You passing this class is based on his opinion, rather than number grades and he ended up only letting one person pass. Other professors even warn people about him. He is incredibly smart but cannot make the information easy to understand and provides no resources.

She was a great organic professor, she was reasonably hard as a professor, but nothing too crazy. If you do your weekly homework and attend problem sessions you will have a blast in her class. She is very caring and respectful.

Had Dr. Wells for Orgo 1 and 2 and she was a great professor. Lectures are easy to follow, exams are straight forward if you study a lot, homework each week but isn't too much. Accommodates students' needs as well.

Dr. Wells is a great professor. She is well knowledgable of the material and makes organic chemistry very easy to understand. I recommend doing the book problems and read her assigned book chapters. I would take her again if I could :)

I took Orgo 1 with Dr.Wells, amd her class is the first time in a while I felt set up for success. She has detailed lecture notes that leave me with a better understanding than the textbook, and the test questions mimic what we do in class, as well as the practice problems in the texbook. You MUST attend class, or you'll be overwhelmed.

Dr. Wells gives you everything you need to excel in this course including plenty of practice tests and problem session sheets that are very similar to the exams. Anyone who does not pass taking her just does not want it bad enough.

I found Dr. Krueger to be a very good lecturer. Definitely read the textbook, it helps significantly with the quizzes. Do practice problems from the textbook. I thought the quizzes were pretty straight forward if you read the textbook.

RUN. She makes Striplin's class seem like sunshine and rainbows! I'd switch to a Monday at 8am to get away from her if I had to. I would rather drink a gallon of colonoscopy prep right before a 3-hour-long final exam instead of taking another class with this woman. If you get stuck with her, go ahead and make an appointment with CAPS to save time!

There was multiple times where she would use google to tell us what were the answers. Sometimes she was very lost with the worksheets and then had the nerve to grade hard on worksheets that she waited till the end of the semester to grade. I would rather drink jeans through a straw and swallow a watermelon whole than retake this.

Terrible. Nitpicks the crap out of everything. Has to Google basic chemistry concepts. Accused me of sharing my prelab with a classmate even though I did not. Literally takes 5-7 business days to reply to emails. Often late, lectures for up to 45 min, and takes it out on us when we can't finish
If possible switch sections to a different instructor.

She seemed ready to work with her students. But, when somebody would ask her for help with a topic in lab, she wouldn't know how to help, was scatterbrained/flustered. I know the lab system is hard but stay away unless you want someone who isn't sure of the course material and will be openly upset at your class for not knowing exactly what to do.
He made the class fun and exciting. He was very hands-on and knowledgeable.
Really awesome professor. Make sure to study up for tests because they held most people back
He made the class fun and exciting. He was very hands-on and knowledgeable.
Really awesome professor. Make sure to study up for tests because they held most people back
He made the class fun and exciting. He was very hands-on and knowledgeable.
Really awesome professor. Make sure to study up for tests because they held most people back
Very nice professor! PCAS before every class, 4 exams then a final. Memorize her notes and the class should be manageable.

She is a pretty average professor. She is not as bad as people say, but she isn't a Dr. McKenzie/Slowey. She does not have problem session, so take that as you will. She does have a very structured lecture outline which is nice, and you do genuinely learn a lot from this class. Make sure your chem 1 knowledge is solid.

Be student who participates and gets to know your professors. It will be extremely helpful for your success in the class. The curriculum is difficult and it takes ALOT of work outside of the classroom to master the material for tests & the HW can be heavy depending on the week. However, she cares for her students and their performance in the class.

The practice questions in class and on the practice exam are nothing like the actual exam. There is no problem session like every other chem professor has. Rushes through the lectures when short on time and doesn't explain things much.

DO NOT take chemistry with her. Can't actually explain any of the concepts in depth. Lost 2 of my exams, before even looking she made it seem like I had faked taking the tests, and had me look through every students exams to find mine because she didn't see it, they were both there. I honestly don't understand how she still has a job.

Really bad. When assignments get turned in she and her TA's are very picky on the answer you put in like lets say its .5397 but the answer is .5340 your not getting an ounce of credit even if you got it right almost nuh uh and most of the exam review is sometimes not even on the test and is pulled from another question or thing you didnt learn DONT

Only reason why I took her was due to the fact she was the only chemistry teacher available. If I had to choose again I would choose someone else then her. She grades poorly and there is no resubmissions for it and when we get answers wrong she doesn't explain why we got it wrong, and then refuses to publish grades after a week notice. Don't take.

Marks assignmnts wrong when correct to see if you check. Losing marks for proper work.

No clear focus on how to submit assignmnts or prgrms to use. Lose marks while learning topics without opportunity to correct mistakes. get asked same question for more marks before having received the correction in the first place. potentially losing more marks.

Took as Hybrid. Doesn't post lecture slides on Canvas. Assignment directions can be confusing if you miss class.Attendance isn't part of the grade. Email responses are slow despite saying she will reply within 48 hours on business days. Tests and the final are 65 percent of grade,so other work barely affects it.

I took the hybrid CHEM-1200 course with this professor. We met once a week and the rest was online. Assignments and attendance barely affect your grade exams determine almost everything. If you miss class, you're out of luck because no lectures or PowerPoints are posted.

For this class … I would Pick a god and pray

He's an amazing lecturer, I had never taken an upper level chemistry course and learned a lot in his class. Take the time to prepare for exams, but he gives practice exams and teaches all information in class. Would DEFINITELY recommend his class to anyone taking CHEM1251.

An entertaining lecturer, you will not get bored listening to him. If you take his class seriously, then success should come. Practice, practice, and more practice. Reading the textbook proved valuable conceptually, and in-class polls are graded on accuracy
Literally loved him so much. I wish I could have him teach me every other chem class I have to take but alas that is not an option. If you can take him, do it.

Actually goated prof. Smart, funny, engaging, and helpful. Go to class and office hours if you want a good grade. Actually the best OAT. Very test heavy, so know your material. The class builds on itself.
absolute goat

He is a really sweet professor, but the work he provides for us for the exams is NOTHING like the actual exams. Our class average on the exams is really low, and sadly, it seems like I must retake the class because of the exams.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU CAN HELP IT. Not only is Professor Shelton rude and has an attitude when you ask questions, but he will not help in any way if you are trying to understand the material better. His exams are insane, not anything like the "study guides" we have. The TAs will be your saving grace and a blessing.

He's overall a good guy but your grade is really only determined on how you did for the exams. Lots and lots of homework that doesn't really go towards your grade at all. TA's don't go over how to solve problems rather than just give you the answer for problem sessions. Overall wouldn't recommend!

Hybrid course. Homework consists of discussions and problem sets that don't affect your grade. Tests are the only assignment that control your grade. Professor himself is very engaging and lenient. Sometimes handwriting is difficult to understand, he is very accessible. TAs give answers when doing problem sets rather than explaining questions.
Overall not a bad class but it would've been nice if he was actually in class for questions. You bassically have to teach yourself and practice otherwise you will indeed fail!!!

Dr Walter was by far one of my favorite professors! He is fun, kind, respectful, responsive and very knowledgeable. He was always down to meet with students and answer questions and you could tell he really cared about your understanding. O chem king for real. If you can take him please.

This professor was ahh sss. I had technical issues and she did not give a f. Like she just gave me a zero. Crazy part is that I handed the lab in before it was due and time to fix it but she was like I already graded so she gave me a zero and did nolt let me resubmit.

This professor by far is the GEM of the chemistry department, even surpassing anyone at UNCC. The way she teaches and her dedication to the difficult subject and her effort to help them learn is MASSIVELY EXTRAORDINARY. Take this course with Prof. Pavel & YOU WILL SUCCEED. TAKE HER !!!!

I had a GREAT time! She may come across as harsh/cold, but DON'T take that personally. Very passionate about chemistry and willing to help. She is funny and will joke around with you. She is worth getting to know, and I would love to take all labs from her forever! She doesn't play favorites. Her pre-lab lectures run long.  Love her!

Great chem professor. Homework is hard but are very useful for tests. She gave extra credit so take advantage of that. Lectures are good and aren't the easiest to follow but will answer any questions thoroughly. Does not require a textbook and she says attendance is mandatory but it really isn't. Take her if you can.

She is passionate about Chemistry and will do her very best to ensure all students understand. ASK QUESTIONS! she will take as much time as needed to explain, she also gives guided notes (not for grades, just for personal use) to make sure you understand key topics from each chapter. She uses the extra practice questions on the tests, so do them.

Great professor! He's pretty tough and holds high standards, but he's also incredibly fair and genuinely wants his students to succeed. He pushes you to do your best and gives some of the most thoughtful advice, especially when it came to developing and refining our proposal documents. One of the most helpful and motivating instructors I've had.
One of the most engaging and supportive professors I've ever had. He cares about student learning and are always available to help!

Dr. Afonin has impressive knowledge and experience in grant/proposal writing. He uses real-life examples and teach you about what a real grant application look like. Speakers from academia, industry and gov lab are invited weekly to share their perspectives for career planning. He will push you hard but I find it very helpful for my career

I must say that Dr. Afonin's teaching style is very interactive and he is excellent in conveying the course materials that I like most. He is very knowledgeable and successful in his field, so he always tries to motivate the students to step out of their comfort zones and face challenges. He always provides constructive feedback on assignments.

Thanks to the intimate class size, we were able to receive incredibly constructive feedback on each of our proposals. The clear and challenging expectations pushed me to develop invaluable grant-writing skills that will be extremely beneficial as a Ph.D. student and in my future career.

Really nice guy, knows what he's talking about. He expects you to read the textbook before coming to lecture, and kind of goes over what was in the book. Uses a lot of example problems during lecture to teach the material. Only downside is he grades by a few things- quiz every friday, 3 exams + final, and one extra credit assignment.

Like any organic chemistry class, it's going to be extremely difficult. Quizzes every Friday, no homework to help boost grade, and 4 exams. Professor is great, easy to talk to, and truly does seem to care about your learning but the exams, in my opinion, are just so hard.

quizzes every week, which were given regardless of how prepared the class was. Tests were difficult and he provided little feedback. He taught the class as if we already know the material, and didn't help in office hours. other classes were insanely ahead of us.
The lecture is boring, I honestly learn more from the textbook. There are no homeworks, quiz every Friday, so exams and quizzes weigh a lot.

really friendly guy, but his lectures aren't helpful at all in terms of content on his tests. a quiz every friday, the way he lectures is not really teaching, more like just telling us what it is. also expects us to have prior knowledge on everything.

Not only is he very rude to his students, The projects in this course are unbelievable and his expectations are insane. there is a special topics project, multiple exams, 2 presentations with 10 page reports, and a closed book final. The professor does not have a canvas page DO NOT take any course with this guy.

#1 OG. Top Dawg.

Very knowledgeable and engaging with students. It's a tough class but as long as you're actively participating Dr. Slowey does the absolute best to help you understand something. So ask questions. Try to keep the same energy because it makes the lectures so much better. Overall definitely one of my favorite professors of all time
Dr Slowey is knowledgeable and explains concepts clearly. He uses examples during lectures. Exams are challenging. Practice tests are provided. He is approachable and office hours are useful.

Good professor, he is clearly VERY passionate about chemistry.

He goes through material very quickly but takes questions before moving on during lectures. Content builds off of previous units in this course so do not get behind. Just study and attend office hours outside of class if you don't understand something and you will be fine.

Funniest and chilliest chemistry professor I have ever had. Goes through lectures quick but provides a lot of opportunity to do practice problems and ask questions. It is important that you go to class with at least a preview of the textbook, so you're not totally lost in class. Practice problems are harder than the actual exams. Easy pass :)

Dr. Slowey is a good professor.  He demonstrated some growing pains like errors to problem solutions, but I wouldn't expect to see this next semester, as this was his first at UNCC.  It is of note that he gives relatable examples and of all the professors I've had, he is by far one of the most approachable, easiest to access, and willing to help.

Dr. Fessler was a great professor and fair grader. His class is based on 10 quizzes and 3 exams and a final. He provided 3 extra 8 points each and practice problems. In his class, you need to read the book and know the contents of the chapter very well by the end of each week. If you lose point go and talk to him with your reasoning.

The course is based on just quiz and test grades both of which are made up of very few questions that were often about very random things discussed in the course. Not nearly enough practice problems were given. Smart guy but doesnt teach in a way that is easily understandable to someone taking the course for the first time.

Professlor is an extremely knowledgeable guy about organic chemistry, but he is not effective at teaching students who do not have as deep of an understanding of chemistry as him. This course is entirely based on quizzes and test (the extra credit is mandatory if you want to pass). Would recommend staying away unless you have a PhD understanding.

Dr. Fessler ran this class exactly how Dr. Walter would. He gives extra credit on each test, gives plenty of practice to do outside of class, and cuts out information from the textbook that he doesn't feel matters. He isn't amazing at lecturing due to his inexperience, but, as a chem major I have had a lot worse!

I do believe Dr. Fessler is a good professor. However, if you are a bio major I would stay away from him. His explanations are very chemistry-heavy and do not relate to biology since that isn't his background. As a chem major myself I have enjoyed his class a lot. He is a strict grader, however, his exams reflect the practice exams given.
Great professor, engaging lectures, at times might be confusing, but very down to earth professor! would take class with her again
Newer professor but seems bored when lecturing. I've also heard that both of her grad students left her lab already in her short time here. Exams are difficult.

Notes are easier to read than Etzkorn's, but exam/final questions are impossible and barely relate to anything discussed in class. Lectures were disorganized and skipped around topics a lot. Practice exams provided (with no answer keys) were nothing like the actual exams we were given. Lectures and textbook did not prepare me for these exams.
Dr. Ge is a new lecturer and is nice and always prepared to teach. Only qualm is that she is prone to making small mistakes at times during her lectures. Hard class! Read that book cover to cover!

The ppl complaining are mad that she isnt the same prof as Troutman. The exams had copy-paste questions from the textbook problems she gave us. Shes a sweet lecturer and curves exams, and weekly canvas quizzes. Shes passionate about her field and knows what shes talking about. I wished her exams werent free response heavy tho. Read the textbook lol

No extra credit offered. Generated email response to all students, showing no effort to make the response personal. Took a month to get back to my emails. Goes fast through lectures and does not have patience with students.

Intro to chem. She is very nice and lets you retake the first 3 exams, they aren't too hard. Although it's an intro class and the topics are very easy, I highly recommend studying due to the memorization of elements, polyatomic ions, etc. Overall a very simple and normal introductory course with a good prof

This is an introductory chem classes and she teaches it like an introductory chem class. She lets you retake the first 3 exams (which is uncommon in the chem department), helps you tremendously in office hours and is very understanding and cares for you as a student. She understands that chemistry doesn't come naturally to everyone and thats okay!

Professor Kant is a very clear and organized instructor. Her assignments were fairly simple, and she thoroughly explains well during her lectures and such. I like how she switches her activities up, so it isn't just lecture all the time. Professor Kant is a lovely individual and does well with being supportive and available to all students.

super laid-back and sweet. I went to her office hours and emailed back and forth a few times she's really nice and accommodating if you build a relationship with her. I've heard from most of my class that it's their hardest class but if you pay attention during lectures she does a great job explaining and it was one of my favorite classes!
If you can avoid having her as a professor, do it.

She does not teach during class. She just hands out worksheets and there are no notes. You are expected to teach yourself the material, and there is a lot of homework. When asking a question her explanations are not helpful. I attended each class and still struggled. Her communication is also not the best. I would not recommend this course.

Hardest class I have ever taken. She does not teach, and you essentially have to do everything on your own. I have never cried over the amount of homework I've had to do until I've been in this class. There is too much work and I believe that we aren't even learning what's supposed to be in the curriculum. I am glad to be leaving this class.

Be prepare to teach yourself!

She's the kind of teacher that make you learn on your own, and if you ask for help it's unbelievably vague. When she does help us,she's more or less teaching to herself, and not to the class.

ALSO,Get ready to take a quiz/test on something you have no clue how to do, AND THEN get the notes on how to do it afterwards.

Awful teacher, doesn't teach at all, just gives busy work and expects everyone to understand. When asked for help literally answered with “No”, if you can avoid her classes, please do. Be prepared to have to have a tutor.
GOATTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT

Condescending mannerisms, ignored emails and then would put you on blast in a course announcement. Grading is very nit picky by the TAs, and LabFlow is a nuisance to success. Lecture slides never made sense, and no leniency on any assignments. Write your due dates down in advance, she does not allow for extension.
She was an amazing lab coordinator and was always willing to help.
Good lab. You be mixing potions.

She answers questions in a manner that is respectful and understandable. Was extremely helpful during the labs and will get your group on track if you get lost. Be prepared to write A LOT during the pre-labs.

Dhiman takes forever to grade assignments and when she does, she'll be extremely tough. She'll eat in the middle of lab and wear AirPods the entire time. Usually she'll be on the phone talking and if you ask her a question, she'll act like you're bothering her. She's little to no help and only assists if Rabinovich is present.
Professor Foley is a good teacher but the class itself is very hard so be prepared!

The topics are given very broadly and he wants you to spend OUTRAGEOUS amounts of time diving deeper into the topics on your own. There are pop quizzes, so show up. The test are written response, and no multiple choose! Don't take if you have more than 4 classes! Also, he says read the book, but also says don't memorize the book…
A lot of work and boring

Don't know why this is even considered a biology lab. You do maybe 2-3 labs actually related to what you're learning in lecture. Didn't receive the grade I wanted because he wasn't willing to accept the fact that we accidentally submitted the wrong document with PROOF that we did it in class and submitted it IN CLASS.

For a one credit lab, you get graded ridiculously harshly. Everything is busy work and even if your questions only require a two sentence answer, be prepared to write a paragraph FOR EVERYTHING or be prepared to get a bad grade. TA was very passive aggressive and for a lab, you have to make your own project. You only actually get to do like 2 labs.

This is quite literally the most ridiculous class I have ever taken. ZERO point in the difficulty level, much less for a 1 credit hour class. Honestly shame on UNCC for even continuing to have this class the way it is because there is absolutely no reason for it to be 8-20 hours of work a week. No experiments only lectures and projects.

Mrs Bates is the best teacher I think I have ever had. She cares so much about her students and our success. Shes very engaging and a great hands on teacher. If i could take her again, I most certainly would
Mrs. Bates is the best!!! She is such a caring and sweet professor. She is very interactive, and loves what she does.

Professor Bates is absolutely amazing! She has set the standard for being a phenomenal educator, you can tell she truly cares about the success of her students. She is very kind & understanding, also love her humor! She makes it easy to get an A in this course. Test 1 & 3 are on the easier side, test 2 is a bit more challenging. Take her!!

She is literally an ANGEL. Lectures make sense, she's so caring in class and over email, everything is explained clearly. Tests are very straightforward. Read the book and review class notes and you'll do great. Attendance is not really mandatory, but you will definitely do better if you go! One of my favorite professors!

I LOVE HER!!! These reviews truly weren't lying. She has to be one of the BEST professors I've ever had. She's extremely sweet and you can tell that she cares a lot about our success. She doesn't try to trick us on exams and makes reading guides for each unit to help us. I'd take her a MILLION times over. Pay attention, engage + attend office hrs.
Pretty funny dude but expects way too much from students
Participation matters. Mr. Goff really cares about his students but his exams require extreme critical thinking and often are very very in-depth with what he goes over in lecture.

His tests are hard for the majority of people, but if you study and ACTUALLY read the textbook and not skim it, you will get a good grade. He is very funny and personable, provides lots of real world examples. Make flashcards on key vocabulary, type down his lecture information. Go to his office hours if you have the time.

He is very funny, and emails you back most of the time.  His lectures are super interactive and honestly are very entertaining. His exams are extremely hard. You need to take ur notes ahead of time, and just annotate them with what he says. The class was difficult to do well in.
Do not take this professor. Textbook heavy and exams are hard!

Overall, a great professor with clear knowledge of the course material. Lectures were well structured and organized in a manner that allowed students to work through and understand the information without becoming overwhelmed.
Her powerpoints are very good and she literally gives you fill in the blank notes that are really helpful. I think it's really hard to fail this class unless you do nothing.
Lectures during the first class of each week, followed up by an activity to cement understanding during the second class.  Dr. Somayaji wants everyone to do well.
Wonderful sweet lady. But the tests are SO hard. Attendance is mandatory. She drops one test grade though

The only reason I didn't get a higher grade in this course was because it took me until the final exam to learn the study methods that worked best for me in this course. Dr. S really cares about her students and wants to see us not only succeed in her class but also in the medical career we pursue. Beware of heavy course material!

Took this class in Fall 2024. Exams are challenging, but if you study the powerpoint slides and follow the note outlines you'll be fine. Reviewing homework questions also helps as sometimes they will appear again on the test albeit reworded. Review sessions with TA are also helpful. Be careful though, because it is a lot of information to memorize.
She is not polite and caring. and doesn't let us to ask the questions in the class.

She's friendly and approachable, but not an effective teacher. The course relied heavily on lectures, and the exams were based on her PowerPoint presentations. The chapter outlined notes she provided were essential for my success.

Take her class if you're a good tester. Outside of this class she is an incredible resource and person, and she's incredibly kind. Unfortunately, the tests are effectively 50 trick questions and there is no way to get extra credit or test corrections for her class,. If you're going try this class under her, only use the PPTs and go to lectures.

Don't worry, Bullock's inability to concisely summarize material in lectures is coupled with her insanely detailed and nightmare inducing exams. This would be less annoying if her exams were based on the textbook, but they're not- and they're also nothing like the homework assignments. Avoid.
Dr. Wells makes her online classes incredibly engaging. Not only was the content interesting, but her video lecture with quizzes also helped to absorb the content.

Overall, she is a nice person, but her late work policy is ridiculous and unforgiving. She accepts no late work, even if you submit 5 minutes after the deadline. She claims she cannot make exceptions to accept late work, but there would be no need for an exception if she didn't have a crazy policy. Don't take if you work or have a busy schedule

This professor honestly isn't very good. She often makes mistakes using technology (in an online class) and I just didn't get much out of the two courses I took with her. If you have a genuine interest in learning biology then I would take another professor's class.
